html:
Exclusive to:

Petrified Trees are a variant of Trees found in Don't Starve Together. They yield Rocks, Flint, or Nitre when mined with a Pickaxe, and therefore are alternatives to Boulders to obtain these resources.

Petrification process happens to all stages of Evergreens, and each Petrified Tree will yield resources based on its size. A default world can start with them in the place of boulders or some of the evergreens in the world will get petrified after a random amount of time. In Don't Starve Together the first petrification takes place between the 44th and 65th day, the following ones take place every 21 to 32 days.

To start the process of Petrification, one random Evergreen or Lumpy Evergreen is chosen as center. All the trees within 9 tile radius, with the condition that the distance between two trees is no further than 2.4 tiles, will be petrified. The number of these trees has to be less than 20% of all the Evergreens before Petrification, otherwise, they will stay as Evergreens.

When petrification starts, the Characters will note a noise coming from the trees nearby (provided they are close enough to the area about to be petrified), and shortly after, all the trees in the area will become petrified.

After petrification, trees can no longer be chopped down with an Axe, but can be mined with a Pickaxe instead. Petrified Trees do not grow, so they will stay in the same stage of growth when they had been transformed.

The following table displays the drops from all sizes of Petrified Evergreens.

Short Normal Tall Old
  • ×1.35
    (1 + 35% to get 1 extra)
  • ×0.25
    (25% to get 1)
  • ×0.25
    (25% to get 1)
  • ×1.75
    (1 + 75% to get 1 extra)
  • ×0.25
    (25% to get 1)
  • ×0.40
    (40% to get 1)
  • ×2.35
    (2 + 35% to get 1 extra)
  • ×0.75
    (75% to get 1)
  • ×0.65
    (65% to get 1)
  • ×1.00
    (25% to get 0, 50% to get 1, 25% to get 2)
  • ×0.75
    (75% to get 1)
  • ×0.25
    (25% to get 1)
"rock_petrified_tree_short" "rock_petrified_tree" "rock_petrified_tree_tall" "rock_petrified_tree_old"

Trivia

  • Petrified Trees are considered to be part of A New Reign, even though they were introduced before the official start of the beta branch.
  • In real life, petrification of trees occurs when a tree gets submerged in mineral-rich water. As the tree rots away, most of the minerals are left behind still in a tree shape. The original wood doesn't become stone, rather, it is replaced.
  • Trees make a faint rock crunching sound as they petrify.
  • If the player(s) aren't careful, wood may become very scarce or even go extinct due to this mechanic.
    • However, due to the Regrowth mechanic, trees will be able to come back from being extinct. Still, it will take at least 50 days for trees to come back, thus making it ideal that the player(s) keep a few pinecones around for emergencies.

Gallery

  • Stages of Growth.
  • A group of Petrified Evergreens.
  • A Totally Normal Tree surrounded by Petrified Evergreens.

"rock_petrified_tree(_short/tall/old)"
“It looks scared stiff.”
–Wilson
“How am I supposed to burn it now?”
–Willow
“Now I punch it apart!”
–Wolfgang
“Now it will never die...”
–Wendy
“LESS ORGANIC. IT'S AN IMPROVEMENT.”
–WX-78
“Should make for an excellent building material.”
–Wickerbottom
“So... senseless.”
–Woodie
“I think it looks much better this way.”
–Maxwell
“My mere presence struck terrör intö their tree-hearts!”
–Wigfrid
“It's all stone and no bark.”
–Webber
“Did someone sculpt that?”
–Warly
“Strong friend!”
–Wormwood
“Solid stone.”
–Winona
“Fweeheehee! A stone cold tree!”
–Wortox
“Rock shaped like tree?”
–Wurt
“What could've turned it to stone?”
–Walter
“Frozen in time.”
–Wanda

In Don't Starve Together the first petrification takes place between the 44th and 65th day, the following ones take place every 21 to 32 days.
To start the process of Petrification, one random Evergreen or Lumpy Evergreen is chosen as center. All the trees within 9 tile radius, with the condition that the distance between two trees is no further than 2.4 tiles, will be petrified. Trivia[edit | edit source]
Petrified Trees are considered to be part of A New Reign, even though they were introduced before the official start of the beta branch.
In real life, petrification of trees occurs when a tree gets submerged in mineral-rich water. As the tree rots away, most of the minerals are left behind still in a tree shape. The original wood doesn't become stone, rather, it is replaced.
Trees make a faint rock crunching sound as they petrify.
If the player(s) aren't careful, wood may become very scarce or even go extinct due to this mechanic.
However, due to the Regrowth mechanic, trees will be able to come back from being extinct. Still, it will take at least 50 days for trees to come back, thus making it ideal that the player(s) keep a few pinecones around for emergencies.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Cây Hóa Đá là một biến thể của Cây được tìm thấy trong Don't Starve Together. Chúng mang lại Đá, Đá lửa hoặc Nitre khi khai thác bằng Cuốc, và do đó là những lựa chọn thay thế cho Đá tảng để có được những tài nguyên này.
